Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The direct measured product linked to GhCYP82L1 manipulation is DMNT.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: DMNT is a terpenoid/homoterpene volatile in the paper.

Enzyme_Name.reason: GhCYP82L1 is identified as a cytochrome P450 enzyme in the abstract.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly describes GhCYP82L1 as a cytochrome P450; family CYP82 is reflected by the gene name itself.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: GhCYP82L1 is directly tested in the DMNT biosynthesis context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: DMNT was quantified from volatiles of herbivore-infested cotton plants, and leaves were sampled after infestation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported: single knockout reduced DMNT, double knockout abolished detectable DMNT, overexpression/co-overexpression increased DMNT, and high-DMNT line L114 affected bug/wasp behavior while high DMNT was toxic in feeding assays.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results show attraction of parasitoid females and toxicity of high DMNT dose to A. lucorum, supporting indirect and direct defense roles.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The Results directly test A. lucorum infestation, MeJA induction, and MeSA induction.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 2 text states expression in overexpression lines was significantly higher at flowering stage than at other stages after infestation.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: DMNT is treated as a homoterpene produced from sesquiterpene precursor oxidation in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results and Discussion show higher DMNT increased attraction of A. lucorum while feeding assays showed toxicity at high doses; Fig. S10 is cited for no adverse growth effects in edited plants.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Discussion explicitly identifies (E)-nerolidol as the precursor substance of DMNT.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are used in the study for this gene or lines involving this gene.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Knockout reduced/abolished DMNT and overexpression increased DMNT emission, showing GhCYP82L1 promotes DMNT biosynthesis/emission.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: GhCYP82L1 is presented as part of the direct biosynthetic route to DMNT rather than an indirect regulator.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: GhTPS14 and GhCYP82L2 functionally interact genetically in co-overexpression/combined knockout contexts, but direct physical protein interaction is not shown.

Primary_Product.reason: The measured final product associated with GhCYP82L1 activity in this paper is DMNT.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Expression was measured in leaves after A. lucorum infestation and was highest at flowering stage in overexpression lines.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports GhCYP82L1 as important for DMNT production, but does not formally test or state rate-limiting status.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper concludes manipulation of DMNT biosynthesis/emission is promising for mirid bug control, and L114 stacking gave strongest increase.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Single knockout reduced but did not eliminate DMNT, while double knockout abolished it, supporting functional redundancy with GhCYP82L2.
